title: Session Audit for Privileged Interactive Access

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
spl:
index=os earliest=-24h (sourcetype=linux:secure OR sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security)
| search process=sshd OR EventCode IN (4624,4647,4778)
| eval session_actor=coalesce(user, Account_Name)
| stats earliest(_time) as start, latest(_time) as end, values(process) as processes by session_actor, src_ip, dest
| eval duration_sec=end-start
value: Captures SSH/RDP session telemetry (logon, channel, disconnect) for AU-14 session audit on administrative jump hosts.
implementation: (1) Enable verbose sshd logging per policy; (2) map EventCode meanings in a lookup; (3) alert on long sessions to sensitive assets; (4) redact passwords never present in logs.
visualization: Timeline (session spans), Table (duration by admin), Bar chart (sessions by jump host)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src, Authentication.dest | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
